frameworks.name: corticostriatal loops
frameworks.one_line: recursive cortex-basal ganglia circuits balance detail acquisition and generalization in category learning

frameworks.name: reward-gated plasticity (basal ganglia) vs reward-shaded plasticity (cortex)
frameworks.one_line: dual-timescale plasticity scheme where fast striatal and slow cortical learning bootstrap each other

frameworks.name: basal ganglia vs medial temporal lobe systems
frameworks.one_line: two memory systems that interact competitively or cooperatively depending on task demands

frameworks.name: midbrain dopaminergic reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: dopamine reward signals gate striatal plasticity during category acquisition

core_question: How do distributed neural systems interact to support category learning in the brain?
key_assumptions: categorization is supported by plasticity across multiple neural systems
fast and slow plasticity coexist in different brain regions
task demands modulate which memory system dominates
manipulations: review across category-learning paradigms varying in feedback and task structure
comparison across novice versus skilled performance stages
key_findings: many systems (neocortex, MTL, basal ganglia, midbrain dopamine) show plasticity during category learning
basal ganglia and MTL interact competitively or cooperatively depending on task demands
corticostriatal loops integrate perceptual, response, and feedback aspects of the task
interactions mediate the shift from novice to skilled performance
interpretations: recursive striatum-cortex bootstrapping balances acquisition of detail with generalization
category learning emerges from coordinated multi-system plasticity, not a single locus
